_readme: APPROVED recipe: every knob pinned (a later generic-default change cannot restyle this look). starcomb resolution: CLI > this file > GENERIC.

notes.starless_denoise: off is the one non-generic pin, measured: the post-stretch vst (the self-flat class's adaptation) crushed this target's object chroma -31..-50% (A/B probe R-G -16.4 -> -8.2; the final render doubles back to -17.5 with off) and smeared the blotchy blue/white galaxy body the user reported — off restores the subtle nebula dust detail at gate PASS (colour 6.0 / grad 2.3 / blotch 1.5 / rings 5.4)
notes.red_halo: the bright-star red halo is the Sigma-180-wide-open veiling-glare signature (R-G plateau +4..+5 counts r~6-38; absent on the same body at 50 mm f/4) — USER VERDICT: honest gear data, never de-fringe it in the render; the fix is acquisition (stop down)
notes.chroma_core: the generic k=4 was the user's pick on this set's k=0/2/4 ladder (neutral look); it does mute some real H-alpha colour on bright targets — re-ladder only with a hypothesis

approved.judgment: results/judgment_lmc_denoise_20260710 (denoise-off vs vstpost control; full-frame lossless pairs)
approved.note: approved for the same reason as the M20 fix: vst was destroying moderate-contrast object colour class-wide
